failed_attempts.description: Increased enemy_aoe weight from 8.0 to 10.0 (+25%) in aligner_target_score
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 100 - Marginal improvement, too small to be meaningful (+0.6%). High variance across seeds (22.80 to 28.81). Similar to Cycle 98 hotspot weight result. Improvement magnitude insufficient compared to historical successes (+1.5% to +41.2%). Decision: revert due to insufficient signal. Success rate: 7/23 = 30.4% (9 consecutive failures).

failed_attempts.description: Reduced teammate_penalty from 6.0 to 4.0 (-33%) in aligner_target_score
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: seed 42 scored 14.41 vs baseline 25.42 (-43.3%)
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 99 - Major regression on first seed test. Lower penalty allowed too much coordination overlap, causing multiple aligners to target same junctions and leaving other areas uncovered. The 6.0 penalty is optimal for spreading aligner coverage. Success rate: 7/22 = 31.8%.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced hotspot_weight from 8.0 to 6.0 (-25%) in aligner_target_score
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 98 - Marginal improvement, too small to be meaningful (+0.4%). High variance across seeds (23.96 to 28.73). Improvement is likely statistical noise rather than true performance gain. Previous successful improvements ranged from +1.5% to +41.2%. Decision: revert due to insufficient improvement magnitude. Success rate: 7/21 = 33.3% (declining from 35%).

Cycle 97 - Regression with extreme variance. Seed 42 crashed -31.9%, seed 44 spiked +43.8%. Lower threshold caused unpredictable target switching behavior. Current 3.0 threshold balances commitment vs flexibility optimally.

Cycle 96 - Regression. Lower claim penalty caused worse performance across all seeds. Opposite direction of previous failed attempt (25→reverted). The existing 12.0 penalty appears optimal for balancing coordination vs duplication.

failed_attempts.description: Increased expansion bonus from 5.0 to 7.0 (+40%) in aligner_target_score
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 95 - Regression. Increasing expansion bonus weight made agents overly aggressive at targeting high-expansion junctions, causing poor performance. Seeds 42 and 44 particularly affected (-24.9% and similar). The existing 5.0 weight appears optimal.

failed_attempts.focus: LLM objective wiring to pressure budgets
failed_attempts.description: Wired LLM objective (expand/defend/economy_bootstrap) through to pressure budget computation
failed_attempts.result: FALSE POSITIVE: avg 27.55 vs baseline 25.42 (+8.4% apparent)
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 94 - False positive. Code analysis shows objective=None without LLM, making Cycle 94 identical to Cycle 91. Local tests run with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY= (no LLM), so new code paths never executed. The +8.4% was statistical variance. Cannot validate LLM-dependent changes with current testing protocol (local=no LLM, tournament=Bedrock LLM). High seed variance observed: seed 42 ranges 14-30.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced late-game HP modifiers: aligner/scrambler 10→5, miner/scout 5→0
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 93 - Regression. Reducing late-game HP modifiers caused significant performance drop, particularly on seed 42 (-29%). Mixed results across seeds (42-44 regressed, 45-46 improved slightly). The existing late-game modifiers appear necessary for proper survival balance after step 2500. Unlike base HP thresholds, late-game modifiers serve a different purpose and cannot be reduced.

failed_attempts.description: Increased network bonus from 0.5 to 1.0 (+100%) to encourage stronger chain-building
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 92 - Major regression. Doubling network bonus weight caused severe performance drop across all seeds tested. May have caused agents to over-prioritize clustering around existing junctions instead of expanding to new territories. Alpha.0's 0.5 weight appears to be the optimal balance.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM prompt with explicit stagnation detection and role-switching directives
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 90 - Regression. Enhanced LLM prompt to detect stagnation and force role changes more aggressively (alpha.0 cyborg architecture parity attempt). Caused performance drop, particularly on seeds 44 (-23%) and 46 (-16%). The more directive prompt may have caused excessive role switching or suboptimal strategy adjustments. Simpler prompt works better.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced scrambler HP threshold from 30 to 25 (-16.7%)
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 88 - Regression. Scrambler HP threshold 25 was too aggressive. Unlike aligner and miner optimizations, scrambler role appears to require the higher threshold of 30. Significant regression on seeds 42 (-18%) and 43 (-22%), with only seed 44 showing improvement. The pattern that worked for aligner and miner doesn't apply to scrambler.

failed_attempts.description: Increased RETREAT_MARGIN from 15 to 20 (+33%) to match alpha.0
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 85 - Regression. RETREAT_MARGIN=20 too conservative, caused agents to retreat too early. Previously tested in cac8e71, now properly reverted. The wider enemy detection (_NEAR_ENEMY_RADIUS=20) already adds +5 margin when near enemy territory, so base margin of 20 is excessive.
Delta Cycle 80 - Testing after codebase reset. Stall threshold 14 regressed in new context (previous testing showed 14 optimal, but other parameters have changed).

failed_attempts.description: Reduced hub camp distance from 3 to 2 (-33%) to require agents to be closer to hub for camping
failed_attempts.notes: Delta Cycle 83 - Regression. Tighter distance requirement reduced hub camping effectiveness, particularly damaging on seed 46. Context matters: with HP threshold at 85 (not 100), distance 3 works better than 2.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced early retreat window from 150 to 120 steps (-20%)
failed_attempts.notes: Delta Cycle 84 - Regression. Shorter early retreat window reduced performance, particularly on seed 46. The longer 150-step window provides better early game flexibility in current context.

last_improvement.description: Increased JUNCTION_AOE_RANGE from 10 to 20 (+100%) to match alpha.0
last_improvement.notes: Cycle 91 - Alpha.0 parity achievement. Wider enemy AOE radius improves agent survival near enemy junctions. Particularly strong on seed 42 (+16.6%). This parameter controls enemy detection range for retreat decisions, targeting, and pressure calculations. New baseline: 25.42.
